objective: Establish a verifiable, idempotent E2E binding between the WRK-C1 work-unit and the Claude provider object runtime so that every dispatch produces exactly one governance transport record and one idempotent Telegram routing event, with no duplicate delivery on retry.
assumptions: provider_status.json merge-conflict markers have been noted; repair_note present confirms BEM-948 fix intent; claude provider is bound as active executor.
Telegram routing is governance-synthetic in this E2E; no live bot token is required for the pass.
task_type 'object_runtime_binding' is absent from role_sequence.json; cycle follows default_development sequence per default_task_type.
Idempotency key is trace_id; a second dispatch with the same trace_id must produce no new Telegram message and no duplicate transport record.
governance/transport/results.jsonl is the canonical transport sink; no other file is written by executor.
steps: Step 1 — Resolve provider_status and confirm executor binding: Read provider_status.json; assert claude entry is reachable. Write a single 'binding_probe' record to governance/transport/results.jsonl with trace_id. Skip write if record with same trace_id and record_type already exists (idempotent).

Step 2 — Execute object runtime binding for WRK-C1: Append 'binding_result' record to results.jsonl: trace_id, cycle_id, from_role=executor, to_role=auditor, status=completed, artifact_path=governance/reports/bem948_p0_binding_object_e2e_20260620T0930Z.md. Check for existing record before appending (idempotent). Commit report and transport append.

Step 3 — Idempotent Telegram routing: Compute idempotency_key=SHA256(trace_id+':'+record_type). Check governance/state/telegram_sent.json for key. Emit Telegram notification only if key is absent; write key to dedup store after successful send. On retry, key present — send skipped, no duplicate.

Step 4 — Hand off to auditor: Append 'handoff' record to results.jsonl (from_role=executor, to_role=auditor). Update role_cycle_state.json active_role=auditor. Idempotent on trace_id+record_type=handoff.
acceptance: governance/transport/results.jsonl contains exactly one record with trace_id=bem948_p0_binding_object_e2e_20260620T0930Z and record_type=binding_result, status=completed — verifiable by grep on the file.

A second identical dispatch (same trace_id) produces zero new transport records and zero new Telegram messages — verifiable by running the dispatch twice and asserting results.jsonl line-count is unchanged after the second run.
risks: HIGH — provider_status.json still has unresolved git merge-conflict markers at runtime; mitigation: executor detects markers, writes blocker record, escalates to curator per fallback_rules.on_role_blocked.

MEDIUM — task_type 'object_runtime_binding' not in role_sequence.json causes orchestrator to fall back to default_development; mitigation: auditor final check required before curator_summary regardless of sequence used.
MEDIUM — telegram_sent.json dedup store does not exist on first run; mitigation: executor creates it as empty object if absent so subsequent runs always find the file.
LOW — results.jsonl concurrent writers may produce duplicate lines (pattern seen in provider_status.json conflict); mitigation: append-only atomic writes enforced by orchestrator cycle locking.
risks.objective: Validate WRK-C1 Claude provider object runtime binding through a live binding-mode E2E cycle, including idempotent Telegram routing, and confirm results are recorded in governance transport without duplication.
assumptions: routing.json executor.active = 'claude' is the authoritative WRK-C1 binding declaration.
provider_status.json contains merge conflict markers from concurrent transport writers; repair is in scope per existing repair_note.
The role sequence for task_type 'object_runtime_binding' is not listed in role_sequence.json and falls back to 'default_development': analyst → auditor → executor → auditor → curator_summary.
Idempotent Telegram routing is enforced by deduplication on (trace_id, record_type) in results.jsonl before any Telegram outbox write.
No production credentials or secrets are required; all Telegram delivery in this cycle is synthetic (send_synthetic mode).
steps: STEP-1 | Repair provider_status.json: remove all git merge conflict markers (<<<<<<< / ======= / >>>>>>>); set claude.last_outcome to the most recent authoritative value and claude.status to a single deterministic string; record repair in repair_note.

STEP-2 | Verify WRK-C1 binding: assert routing.json executor.active == 'claude' and that provider_status.json (post-repair) claude entry exists; if claude.status == 'failed', assert fallback_chain activates gpt_codex as reserve per role_sequence.json fallback_rules.on_provider_limit.

STEP-3 | Execute binding-mode E2E cycle: dispatch bem948 through default_development sequence (analyst → auditor → executor → auditor → curator_summary) using trace_id bem948_p0_binding_object_e2e_20260620T0930Z; each role hand-off appends one record to governance/transport/results.jsonl.

STEP-4 | Validate idempotent Telegram routing: replay the same dispatch with identical trace_id; confirm results.jsonl contains no new duplicate entries keyed on (trace_id, record_type); confirm no duplicate Telegram outbox payload is generated.

STEP-5 | Commit and report: commit changed files (governance/reports/bem948_p0_binding_object_e2e_20260620T0930Z.md, governance/transport/results.jsonl, governance/state/provider_status.json if repaired); record commit SHA in transport entry; return result to auditor.
acceptance: AC-1: provider_status.json contains zero git merge conflict markers after STEP-1, and claude.status is a single deterministic string value ('active' or 'failed') with no conflicting hunks.

AC-2: governance/transport/results.jsonl contains exactly one record with record_type='execution' and trace_id='bem948_p0_binding_object_e2e_20260620T0930Z' after STEP-3, and a second identical dispatch in STEP-4 adds zero additional records with the same (trace_id, record_type) pair.
risks: RISK-1: Concurrent workflow runners may re-introduce merge conflict markers into provider_status.json before the repaired version is committed, causing a write race on the same file.
RISK-2: claude.status is currently 'failed' per the last recorded outcome; the live binding E2E may silently route to the gpt_codex reserve without surfacing a clear WRK-C1 binding failure signal.

RISK-3: Idempotent dedup keyed on (trace_id, record_type) breaks if a legitimate auditor rejection triggers a second executor run for the same trace_id; the dedup key must include an 'attempt' counter to distinguish retries from replays.
RISK-4: results.jsonl append-only model has no atomic write guarantee under parallel GitHub Actions runners; simultaneous appends may interleave lines and corrupt JSONL parsing.
risks.objective: Verify and validate end-to-end binding of the Claude provider as a live runtime object for WRK-C1, including idempotent Telegram routing delivery under BEM-948 P0 constraints.
assumptions: The Claude provider object binding interface is authoritative in governance/state/routing.json (executor.active=claude, executor.primary=claude); provider_status.json is supplementary state and does not override routing.json declarations.

Telegram routing uses the existing outbox mechanism with deduplication keyed on trace_id; a second dispatch with the same trace_id must not produce a second outbox entry (idempotency guaranteed at the routing layer, not the caller).

WRK-C1 is the active Claude executor instance as declared in routing.json; the binding probe must assert this value directly rather than inferring from provider_status.json, which currently contains merge-conflict markers and last_outcome=failure.

provider_status.json merge-conflict markers are a pre-existing transport-writer race condition (9 conflict markers detected in results.jsonl as well) and do not invalidate the routing declaration; resolving them is a prerequisite step, not a separate task.

The task type object_runtime_binding follows the default_development role sequence (analyst -> auditor -> executor -> auditor -> curator_summary) per role_sequence.json; executor acts on auditor-approved scope only.
steps: STEP-1 Baseline cleanup: Resolve pre-existing git merge-conflict markers in governance/state/provider_status.json and governance/transport/results.jsonl to establish clean, parseable state before binding probes run. Retain the most-recent post-conflict value for each key; record the repair commit SHA.

STEP-2 Binding probe: Assert routing.json executor.active == 'claude'. Dispatch a minimal role cycle step (task_type=object_runtime_binding) tagged with trace_id=bem948_p0_binding_object_e2e_20260620T0930Z through the Claude provider object. Capture and log: status, provider, cycle_id, binding_mode=live_object, completed_at.

STEP-3 Idempotency probe: Re-dispatch the identical role cycle step with the same trace_id. Assert that governance/transport/results.jsonl gains exactly one new record (not two), that the Telegram outbox contains no duplicate entry for this trace_id, and that the second call returns the cached result rather than a new dispatch. Log idempotency verdict.

STEP-4 State write: Append one verified result record {trace_id, cycle_id, role='executor', provider='claude', status, binding_mode='live_object', telegram_idempotent, completed_at} to governance/transport/results.jsonl.

STEP-5 Report and handoff: Write executor report to governance/reports/bem948_p0_binding_object_e2e_20260620T0930Z.md including checklist, files changed, checks run, and commit SHA. Signal auditor role for final review per role_sequence default_development flow.
acceptance: ACCEPT-1 Object binding confirmed: routing.json executor.active == 'claude' AND the binding probe (STEP-2) returns status=success with trace_id=bem948_p0_binding_object_e2e_20260620T0930Z, provider=claude, and binding_mode=live_object.

ACCEPT-2 Idempotent Telegram routing verified: dispatching the same trace_id twice (STEP-3) produces exactly one entry appended to results.jsonl and exactly one Telegram outbox record for this trace_id — no duplicate delivery, no second dispatch side-effect.
risks: RISK-1 provider_status.json merge-conflict markers (detected) cause JSON parse failures in downstream consumers reading provider state before binding; mitigated by resolving markers in STEP-1 as an explicit prerequisite — if STEP-1 is skipped, STEP-2 binding assertion may read corrupt state.

RISK-2 provider_status.json last_outcome=failure for claude may trigger role_sequence fallback to gpt_codex reserve per routing.json policy (switch_after_failures=2), bypassing the Claude object binding test entirely; mitigated by asserting routing.json executor.active directly and not relying on provider_status outcome field for binding decisions.

RISK-3 results.jsonl merge-conflict markers (9 detected) corrupt JSONL integrity and may cause automated consumers to miscount or skip records; mitigated in STEP-1. If not resolved before STEP-4, the append will succeed but file will remain unparseable as strict JSONL.
risks.objective: Establish and verify live object runtime binding between WRK-C1 and the Claude provider executor for BEM-948 P0, confirming end-to-end cycle delivery with idempotent Telegram routing.
assumptions: routing.json v6 designates claude as the active executor provider; no provider switch is required unless the live probe returns failure.
task_type 'object_runtime_binding' is not listed in role_sequence.json; it maps to the 'default_development' sequence: analyst → auditor → executor → auditor → curator_summary.
provider_status.json contains pre-existing unresolved git merge conflict markers (3 blocks); these must be resolved before the cycle executes to avoid divergent state.
role_cycle_state.json last cycle (bem550-6-full-internal-contour-smoke) is CLOSED/PASS; a new cycle may be opened under trace_id bem948_p0_binding_object_e2e_20260620T0930Z.
Telegram routing uses the existing synthetic outbox adapter; idempotency is enforced by trace_id deduplication on the consumer side before delivery.
steps: STEP 1 — Provider probe and WRK-C1 binding verification: confirm routing.json executor.active == 'claude'; run a live provider health probe; log probe result to governance/transport/results.jsonl with status 'binding_verified' or trigger fallback to gpt_codex per routing.json executor.fallback_chain.

STEP 2 — Resolve pre-existing merge conflict markers: clean governance/state/provider_status.json by keeping the most recent entry (2026-06-20T09:19:35Z) and removing all conflict markers; commit with reference to BEM-948 before starting the live cycle.

STEP 3 — Execute default_development role sequence under trace_id: run analyst → auditor → executor → auditor → curator_summary; write each role result record to governance/transport/results.jsonl and update governance/state/role_cycle_state.json with cycle_id and current active_role after each step.

STEP 4 — Idempotent Telegram routing verification: submit the outbox notification twice with the same trace_id bem948_p0_binding_object_e2e_20260620T0930Z; assert the Telegram consumer delivers exactly one message; append record to governance/transport/results.jsonl with field telegram_idempotency='pass' or 'fail'.

STEP 5 — Curator summary and cycle closure: write the final curator_summary record to governance/transport/results.jsonl with status 'completed'; update governance/state/role_cycle_state.json with curator_status='CLOSED', completed_at, and bem948_smoke='PASS' or 'FAIL'.
acceptance: ACCEPT-1: governance/transport/results.jsonl contains exactly one record with trace_id == 'bem948_p0_binding_object_e2e_20260620T0930Z' and status == 'completed' after the full default_development cycle; no duplicate or partial records for this trace_id.
ACCEPT-2: Telegram idempotency verified — sending the same trace_id twice results in exactly one delivered message; results.jsonl contains a record with telegram_idempotency == 'pass' for this trace.
ACCEPT-3: governance/state/provider_status.json is free of git conflict markers and reflects the outcome (pass or fallback) of the BEM-948 live provider probe.
risks: RISK-1: provider_status.json has three unresolved git merge conflict markers from concurrent transport writers; resolving them may surface divergent state snapshots requiring manual curator decision.

RISK-2: claude provider shows status='failed' for the three most recent traces; if the live probe fails again, executor must switch to gpt_codex per routing.json fallback_chain — this changes the active provider mid-cycle.

RISK-3: task_type 'object_runtime_binding' is not explicitly defined in role_sequence.json; defaulting to 'default_development' may not match the intended governance sequence and should be confirmed by curator before execution.
RISK-4: results.jsonl contains stash conflict markers that break JSON parsing for downstream consumers; the file must be repaired alongside provider_status.json before transport records are written.
RISK-5: role_cycle_state.json has not been updated since 2026-05-17; stale state may cause the role orchestrator to misread cycle boundaries if it does not verify cycle_id before dispatching.
